user.title: Configure GrubStation
user.description:
If you need help with the configuration have a look here: {documentation_url}

A secure, random Webhook ID will be automatically generated for you in the next step to protect your system from unauthorized access.

webhook_info.title: Generated Webhook ID
webhook_info.description:
Here is your securely generated Webhook ID. **Please copy this now as it will not be shown again.**

```text
{webhook_id}
```

For full instructions on configuring and installing the Grub OS Reporter, visit the [Grub OS Reporter]({daemon_url}) repository.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure GrubStation
reconfigure.description: **Warning:** Regenerating the webhook ID will break the connection with your existing Grub OS Reporters until they are updated with the new ID.

reconfigure_webhook_info.title: New Webhook ID
reconfigure_webhook_info.description:
Here is your securely generated new Webhook ID. **Please copy this now as it will not be shown again.**

```text
{webhook_id}
```

Remember to update all your Grub OS Reporters with this new ID.

For full instructions on configuring and installing the Grub OS Reporter, visit the [Grub OS Reporter]({daemon_url}) repository.

view_webhook.title: Webhook ID
view_webhook.description:
Your current Webhook ID is:

```text
{webhook_id}
```

*Note: To generate a new ID, use the **Reconfigure** option in the integration menu.*

host_config.title: Configure {host_name}
host_config.description:
Define an action to gracefully shut down the remote host. If no action is provided, the integration will attempt to use the built-in daemon shutdown command.

**Note:** You can also manually adjust the network settings below for troubleshooting, but these will be overwritten the next time the host checks in.

data_description.turn_off_action: An optional list of Home Assistant actions to perform for shutdown. If left as 'Add Action' (blank), the integration will use the built-in daemon shutdown (requires Address and API Key).
data_description.address: The network address that HA will ping to check to see if the host is online. This can be an IP address or hostname. If left blank, there will not be any ping checks.
data_description.broadcast_address: The IP address to send magic packets to. Defaults to `255.255.255.255` (`ff02::1` for ipv6) and is typically not changed.
data_description.broadcast_port: The port to send magic packets to. Defaults to `9` and is typically not changed.

abort.no_hosts: No hosts found. Please make sure your Grub OS Reporter is properly configured and has checked in at least once before trying to configure options.

send_turn_on_command.name: Send turn on command
send_turn_on_command.description: Sends a Wake-on-LAN magic packet to a host.

mac.name: MAC address
mac.description: The MAC address of the host to wake up.

broadcast_address.name: Broadcast address
broadcast_address.description: The IP address to send the magic packet to.

broadcast_port.name: Broadcast port
broadcast_port.description: The port to send the magic packet to.

send_turn_off_command.name: Send turn off command
send_turn_off_command.description: Sends a shutdown command to the Grub OS Reporter daemon on a remote host.

address.name: Address
address.description: The network address (IP or hostname) of the host.

port.name: Port
port.description: The port the daemon is listening on. Defaults to 8081.

api_key.name: API key
api_key.description: The API key required to authenticate with the daemon.
